HomePage.headline: Build geography memory with topic-based map quizzes.
HomePage.subheadline: MapQuiz.pro covers broad map quiz intent with focused landing pages, instant answer feedback, and fast replay loops for world regions, countries, and classroom-style practice.
HomePage.startWithBiggest: Start with the biggest search intents
HomePage.startWithBiggestDesc: These landing pages capture the broadest geography and map quiz queries while still leading users into the same replayable interaction loop.
HomePage.chooseContinents: Choose a continent and drill it fast
HomePage.chooseContinentsDesc: Each continent page narrows the country pool, increases replay value, and gives searchers a clearer page match.
HomePage.goDeeper: Go deeper with country-specific map quizzes
HomePage.goDeeperDesc: Country pages turn the same engine into narrower study products for state capitals, province capitals, major cities, and landmarks.
HomePage.jumpStraight: Jump straight into focused country drills
HomePage.jumpStraightDesc: These subtopic pages surface the most rankable long-tail intents across Japan, Germany, Canada, Australia, France, the UK, Italy, and Spain.
HomePage.featuredCampaignsDesc: Start with ready-made adventure runs, then remix them into your own version with one short prompt.
HomePage.popularGuidesDesc: Use these articles to connect quiz play with study routines, classroom use, and broader map-memory habits.
HomePage.commonQuestionsDesc: This FAQ layer captures broader search intent around geography games, map practice, and classroom-friendly use.

CampaignsPage.headline: Play map campaigns, not only quiz lists.
CampaignsPage.subheadline: These official runs package the current quiz engine into short, replayable missions with built-in remix prompts and safer defaults for first-time players.
CampaignsPage.featuredTitle: Start with the three official runs
CampaignsPage.featuredDescription: Each campaign is built from the existing quiz pool, so the geography stays grounded while the mission framing becomes more game-like.

CampaignPage.missionPlanDescription: This first slice still uses the current quiz engine underneath. The campaign layer packages those topics into a clearer run structure.
CampaignPage.remixDescription: The next implementation slice can turn these examples into generated campaign variants without forcing a full editor.
CampaignPage.remixStudioDescription: Turn one line into a playable campaign draft. When the server has an OpenAI key, this panel upgrades the copy live. Otherwise it falls back to the local draft so the flow still works.
CampaignPage.remixPromptPlaceholder: Make this a treasure hunt across island routes.
CampaignPage.requestFailed: The live remix request failed. Falling back to the local draft.
CampaignPage.relatedQuizTopicsDescription: These current quiz pages are the factual source behind this campaign.

QuizzesPage.headline: Explore every map quiz in one place.
QuizzesPage.subheadline: World quizzes, continent drills, country hubs, and focused regional practice — all in one library.
QuizzesPage.progressDescription: Track which topics you've practiced, where you're making mistakes, and what to focus on next.
QuizzesPage.countryMapQuizzesDesc: Start with any country for a full quiz covering its regions, cities, and landmarks.
QuizzesPage.featuredCountryDeepDivesDesc: Jump straight into focused practice for a specific country — states, prefectures, provinces, and more.
QuizzesPage.focusedRegionalPracticeDesc: Narrow your practice to specific regions, capitals, cities, or landmarks within a country.
QuizzesPage.learningPathsDescription: Follow a guided route from broad quizzes into deeper geography drills without guessing what to practice next.
QuizzesPage.guideLibraryDescription: These blog articles cover long-tail geography game intent around routines, memorization, and classroom use.
QuizzesPage.faqDescription: Answer broad product and learning questions while keeping users connected to playable map quiz pages.

Start.worldModeDescription: Guess countries on a world map
Start.countryModeDescription: Pick a country and guess its regions
Start.chooseCountryDescription: Tap any country to start instantly

GameBar.campaignBriefingLine: Briefing the route. Start the run to receive the first live target.
GameBar.campaignActiveLine: Find {target}, then lock the region to advance the story beat.
GameBar.campaignSelectedLine: Locked {selection}. Submit to resolve this mission beat.
GameBar.terrainRules: Terrain rules: read the relief, lock the region, send fast.
GameBar.flashRules: Flash rules: hear the target, lock the spot, send fast.
GameBar.noSatelliteHintsDescription: Refills automatically tomorrow, or purchase more credits

Result.shareScore: Today's challenge is done. Share your score.
Result.masteryTitle: How your map practice is trending
Result.masteryDesc: This local summary now updates after every finished run, so you can decide whether to push your strongest topic or clean up missed locations.
Result.grade0Message: Every attempt builds your mental map.
Result.grade50Message: You're getting the hang of it.
Result.shareTextDaily: I scored {accuracy}% on today's MapQuiz.pro daily challenge. Can you beat it?
Result.shareTextDailyWithStreak: I scored {accuracy}% on today's MapQuiz.pro daily challenge and kept a {streak}-day streak alive. Can you beat it?
Result.shareTextTopic: I scored {accuracy}% on {topic} in MapQuiz.pro. See if you can beat my result.
Result.shareCopyDescription: Result link and message copied to clipboard.
Result.shareableResultTitle: Turn this result into a challenge link.
Result.shareableResultDescription: Share link keeps the result reproducible.
Result.campaignEvalPursuitRecoveryTitle: The cargo is back on the map, but the trail is still messy
Result.campaignEvalPursuitRecoveryBody: You dragged Lost Shipping Route home at {accuracy}% accuracy. Morning Star is no longer gone, but the ledger still has weak links worth replaying.
Result.campaignEvalPursuitSteadyTitle: The supply trail is readable again
Result.campaignEvalPursuitSteadyBody: You closed the pursuit at {accuracy}% accuracy. The forged route is mostly untangled now, and one more pass could turn the recovery into a cleaner takedown.
Result.campaignEvalPursuitStrongTitle: The smugglers lost control of the route
Result.campaignEvalPursuitStrongBody: You held the chase together at {accuracy}% accuracy. The handoffs stayed sharp, the buyer trail did not shake you, and Morning Star finished the run under pressure.
Result.campaignEvalPursuitEliteTitle: Morning Star came home under your command
Result.campaignEvalPursuitEliteBody: You cleared the pursuit at {accuracy}% accuracy with a best streak of {streak}×. That run reads like a proper recovery operation, not a lucky interception.
Result.campaignEvalCommandRecoveryTitle: The frontier held, but only just
Result.campaignEvalCommandRecoveryBody: You kept Border Crisis Command alive at {accuracy}% accuracy. The Gray Wall Accord survived the night, though several pressure points still need a cleaner command read.
Result.campaignEvalCommandSteadyBody: You stabilized the command net at {accuracy}% accuracy. The false alarms are mostly under control now, and another pass would tighten the frontline decisions further.
Result.campaignEvalCommandStrongTitle: Command kept the panic from spreading
Result.campaignEvalCommandStrongBody: You ran the crisis board at {accuracy}% accuracy and kept the frontier coherent. The convoys moved, the beacons held, and the council had room to breathe.
Result.campaignEvalCommandEliteTitle: The accord survived because the orders held
Result.campaignEvalCommandEliteBody: You finished the command campaign at {accuracy}% accuracy with a best streak of {streak}×. That was not just a win on the map; it was a full-night command performance.
Result.campaignEvalEscapeRecoveryTitle: The ship found air, but the storm was close
Result.campaignEvalEscapeRecoveryBody: You pulled Whiteout Escape through at {accuracy}% accuracy. Polaris Six is out, though the corridor still shows the kind of hesitation a second run could clean up.
Result.campaignEvalEscapeSteadyTitle: The corridor stayed open long enough
Result.campaignEvalEscapeSteadyBody: You held the escape route together at {accuracy}% accuracy. The beacons lined up, the storm edge gave way, and the ship reached clearer water with room to spare.
Result.campaignEvalEscapeStrongTitle: You made the storm chase your route
Result.campaignEvalEscapeStrongBody: You cleared the whiteout at {accuracy}% accuracy and kept your bearings through the worst visibility. Polaris Six never fully lost the line once you took control.
Result.campaignEvalEscapeEliteTitle: Polaris Six cut straight through the white wall
Result.campaignEvalEscapeEliteBody: You escaped at {accuracy}% accuracy with a best streak of {streak}×. That run feels less like survival and more like mastering the storm on its own ground.
Result.campaignEvalRecoveryBody: You closed the run at {accuracy}% accuracy. Replay this campaign once more to clean up weak spots and build a steadier route.
Result.campaignEvalSteadyBody: This run held together at {accuracy}% accuracy. One more pass can turn this into a cleaner campaign finish.
Result.campaignEvalStrongBody: You kept the campaign under control at {accuracy}% accuracy. Your read stayed reliable through the pressure spikes.
Result.campaignEvalEliteBody: You closed this run at {accuracy}% accuracy with a best streak of {streak}×. This remix is ready to share as a serious challenge.
Result.campaignStoryEndingTitle: What this run actually leaves behind
Result.campaignStoryEndingRecoveryIntro: This was more salvage than domination, so the ending reads like aftermath rather than celebration.
Result.campaignStoryEndingSteadyIntro: You stabilized the run, so the ending lands more like a measured debrief.
Result.campaignStoryEndingStrongIntro: This run held together cleanly, so the ending can read like a proper close.
Result.campaignStoryEndingEliteIntro: You controlled the full run, so the ending now lands like a real campaign finale.
Result.nextQuizIdeasDescription: Keep the session going with closely related map quizzes.

QuizSections.popularDesc: The broadest geography and map quiz landing pages.

world-map-quiz.title: World Map Quiz
world-map-quiz.description: Practice locating countries on a clean world map with instant distance feedback and fast replay loops.
world-map-quiz.seoTitle: World Map Quiz — Interactive Geography Game
world-map-quiz.seoDescription: Test your world geography knowledge. Guess countries on an interactive map with instant feedback.
world-map-quiz.searchIntent: Broad geography quiz and map quiz searches
benefits: Immediate submit feedback with visible correct-vs-guessed positions
Distance-based grading that rewards close answers
Fast restart loop for repeated map memory practice

faq.question: Who is this quiz for?
faq.answer: Students, trivia players, and anyone training geography recall.

faq.question: Is this good for school geography practice?
faq.answer: Yes. It focuses on one continent, so it works well for classroom drills and memorization.

MasteryDashboard.title: Mastery dashboard
MasteryDashboard.description: Track your geography practice with one consistent metrics layer, then jump into the most useful next session.

bestMomentum.summary: Your strongest topic so far with {accuracy}% average accuracy across {plays, plural, one {# session} other {# sessions}}.
bestMomentum.empty: Finish a few quiz sessions and this panel will surface your strongest topic automatically.

dailyHabit.doneToday: Today is already marked complete. Keep the loop going with another topic or review your misses.
dailyHabit.notDoneToday: You have not closed today's challenge yet. A quick run here keeps the habit active.

needsReview.uniqueMissesWaiting: You still have {count} unique missed locations waiting here.
needsReview.empty: Wrong answers will start filling this panel automatically once you miss a few locations.

recentMomentum.title: Recent momentum
recentMomentum.description: Use your latest sessions to decide whether to repeat a strong topic or clean up your misses.

RecentPracticePanel.title: Recent practice
RecentPracticePanel.description: Pick up where you left off with your latest topic pages.

MistakesReviewPanel.title: Mistakes review
MistakesReviewPanel.description: Come back to the places you missed and replay them in a focused map session.

GamePage.title: Game session
GamePage.description: Play the current map quiz session.
GamePage.terrainInstruction: Read the land first, then trust the terrain and send the guess.
GamePage.flashInstruction: Tap fast, trust your map read, then send the guess.
GamePage.microRegionInstruction: This small target is marked with an assist point. Tap the small map dot directly.
GamePage.terrainHintHelper: Start with the cue only. Reveal more terrain context if the read is still fuzzy.
GamePage.sessionFollowupDescription: Use these related quiz pages to stay inside the topic network and build more internal depth per visit.
GamePage.resumePracticeDescription: Keep returning users close to the next relevant topic instead of ending the session at the game page.

QuizTopicPage.partOfDescription: This is a focused drill within a broader country quiz. Go back for the full set.
QuizTopicPage.playDescription: Click a location on the map when prompted. Your score updates as you go.
QuizTopicPage.playDescriptionWithCategories: Click a location on the map when prompted. Your score updates as you go. This set focuses on {categories}.
QuizTopicPage.keepExploringDescription: Try related quizzes to broaden your geography knowledge and build on what you've already practiced.
QuizTopicPage.relatedGuidesDescription: Use these articles when you want more study structure around this quiz topic.

BlogPage.headline: Guides that connect map quiz play to real geography practice.
BlogPage.subheadline: Use these articles to build study routines, classroom flows, and stronger long-term map memory around MapQuiz.pro.
BlogPage.featuredDescription: Start with the strongest evergreen articles tied directly to the quiz network.
BlogPage.libraryDescription: Browse long-tail geography game content, study tips, and classroom-friendly map quiz ideas.
BlogPage.relatedArticlesDescription: Keep following nearby search intent without leaving the quiz network.
BlogPage.relatedQuizTopicsDescription: Move from reading into interactive practice with these linked map quizzes.
BlogPage.faqCtaDescription: The FAQ page answers broader questions about map quiz practice, classroom use, and how to structure geography review.

FaqPage.headline: Answers to common questions about MapQuiz.pro and geography quiz practice.
FaqPage.subheadline: Use this page to understand how the game works, how to study with it, and how to connect informational questions back to playable quiz pages.

Footer.description: Interactive geography quizzes, blog guides, and FAQ content built to turn map practice into a repeatable study loop.
Footer.contentDescription: Use the blog and FAQ as stable entry points into the quiz network, whether you are studying solo or teaching geography in class.
Footer.tagline: Built for geography memory, replayable practice, and long-tail map learning.
